what_it_does: Extracts a leading YAML frontmatter block from a markdown string and returns it as a dict, using PyYAML when available and a minimal line parser as a fallback; exposes the result as a deterministic code-side lifter named `frontmatter`.
observations: The docstring frames the file as a SEED lifter and a `produced_by:"code"` projection, explicitly avoiding model calls.
`_extract` is private, fail-soft, and returns `{}` when no frontmatter is found or parsing fails.
It attempts to import PyYAML and falls back to a simple `key: value` line parser if PyYAML is unavailable or fails.
The end-of-frontmatter search looks for `\n---` after the opening `---`.
A top-level `LIFTER` dict registers the lifter with `id`, `extract`, and `desc`.
The docstring states the lifter `id` must equal the file stem (`frontmatter`).
standards: Dual-parser strategy: full YAML when possible, minimal deterministic fallback otherwise.
Treats structural absence of frontmatter as a valid extraction result rather than an error.
Explicitly designed as the code half of a projection split, with no model involvement.
conventions: Private helper prefixed with underscore plus a public `LIFTER` registry descriptor.
Docstring includes architectural context (C9.2 floor, lifter registry, AGENTS.md).
concerns: The fallback line parser is naive: it splits on the first `:` and cannot handle nested structures, quoted values with colons, or multi-line values.
End-marker detection (`\n---`) may miss frontmatter terminators with trailing whitespace or CRLF line endings.
No validation that the extracted `id` actually matches the file stem at runtime.
notes: A small, dependency-optional frontmatter extractor that doubles as a registry-facing code lifter, with a deliberate fail-soft design.
questions: What is the exact meaning and enforcement of the C9.2 floor mentioned in the docstring?
What is the complementary `produced_by:"model"` projection that pairs with this code projection?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it deterministically extracts leading YAML frontmatter and exposes it as a lifter.
intention_signals: Provide a lightweight, always-available frontmatter reader that can participate in a larger lifter pipeline as a pure-code substrate, degrading gracefully when full YAML parsing is unavailable.
novelty: The explicit two-tier parser (PyYAML plus a hand-rolled minimal fallback) and the framing of absent frontmatter as a valid extraction rather than an error.
contribution: A deterministic frontmatter extraction function and a self-describing `LIFTER` descriptor that a fused system can keep for code-side parsing of markdown metadata.
summary_for_embedding: `lifters/frontmatter.py` is a deterministic code-side lifter that extracts the leading `---`-delimited YAML frontmatter from a markdown string and returns it as a dict, falling back to a minimal line parser when PyYAML is unavailable.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to provide a model-free, fail-soft structural extractor for YAML frontmatter that registers itself as a lifter named `frontmatter`.

symbols.does: Deterministically extracts a leading YAML frontmatter block from a string and returns it as a dict, with a soft fallback to a minimal line parser.
symbols.description: `_extract` trims the input, checks for an opening `---`, locates the closing `---`, and attempts to parse the intervening block with PyYAML; if PyYAML is missing or parsing fails, it falls back to splitting lines on the first colon into a flat key/value dict. It returns `{}` for non-string input, missing frontmatter, or any parsing failure, treating absence as a valid extraction result.
